decisions.summary: Fix per file-class, not one-liner: `.beads/issues.jsonl` → built-in `merge=union` (zero config); `.pan/specs|continues|records` single-JSON state → `merge=ours` + wired `merge.ours.driver true`. Confirmed by the operator's issue comment that union corrupts single-JSON objects.

Use `union` for beads, NOT a real `merge.beads.driver`: `bd` has no merge-driver subcommand (`bd merge` is unknown; only `merge-slot`/`duplicates` exist), so the named `beads` driver can never be configured. union is the only viable conflict-free strategy and is built-in/config-free.

Use `merge=ours` with `git config merge.ours.driver true` (the documented git recipe — `true` exits 0, keeping the current branch's whole file) rather than a bespoke timestamp-aware script driver. Safe because the on-main pan-dir auto-commit owns these files and re-stamps them from sources of truth; a transiently stale-but-valid side self-heals.

Add a `pan doctor` guard + unit test that fails on any inert merge attribute (a `.gitattributes` `merge=<driver>` whose `merge.<driver>.driver` is unset and which is not a git built-in union/text/binary). This is the root-cause fix for PAN-1841's silent inertness — it makes the bug class loud and detectable.

Backfill must be UNCONDITIONAL: `sync.ts:329` sets git config only inside the beads-DB-missing `catch`, so it never re-runs on already-initialized repos. The new `ensurePanMergeDrivers(config.path)` call runs for every project in the `pan sync` loop so one `pan sync` heals all existing primary repos.

Extend `merge=ours` coverage to `.pan/records/*.json` (42 tracked) in addition to the `.pan/specs` (233) and `.pan/continues` (147) named in the issue — records are the same single-JSON, per-issue, auto-committed class and conflict identically.

Implemented only the two beads scoped to this issue: (1) switch .beads/issues.jsonl to merge=union in .gitattributes with updated comments; (2) 4 Vitest tests proving union works cleanly and guarding the invariant. Remaining beads from the full PRD (merge=ours for .pan/ state, ensurePanMergeDrivers, pan doctor guard) are separate future work.

hazards.summary: Re-creating the PAN-1841 inert-attribute bug: declaring `merge=ours` in `.gitattributes` without wiring `merge.ours.driver` makes git silently fall back to default merge (no error).
hazards.mitigation: The `merge=ours` attribute bead (vbrief-ours-attr) is `blocks`-gated behind the driver-config helper bead, and the doctor guard bead fails CI/doctor if any non-built-in `merge=` attribute lacks its driver. Land config before/with the attribute.

hazards.summary: `merge=ours` on `git merge origin/main` keeps the LOCAL (main) version and discards origin/main's copy of that per-issue file, which could drop a genuinely newer status from another machine.
hazards.mitigation: Acceptable: the pan-dir auto-commit re-derives and re-commits state from sources of truth (GitHub, records, transcripts) on the next flush, so a stale side self-heals. Never corrupts JSON (unlike union). Documented in NFR-2.

hazards.summary: Scope creep: a blanket `*.json merge=union` would corrupt every single-JSON `.pan` state file.
hazards.mitigation: union is scoped ONLY to `.beads/issues.jsonl`. The `.pan` single-JSON families use `merge=ours`. Enforced by the exact `.gitattributes` patterns in the plan.

hazards.summary: Config writes can fail (read-only repo, missing git). The four existing `git config beads.role contributor` sites swallow errors (`.catch(() => {})`).
hazards.mitigation: `ensurePanMergeDrivers` must be idempotent and best-effort (non-fatal on failure), matching the existing sites (NFR-3). The doctor guard surfaces any repo where the driver did not land.

hazards.summary: The conflict storm occurs on the PRIMARY main worktree (flywheel `git merge origin/main`), not in agent workspaces. Wiring config only at worktree creation would NOT heal the primary repo.
hazards.mitigation: Backfill bead wires `ensurePanMergeDrivers` into the per-project `pan sync` loop (cwd = primary repo path), which is the heal point for primary repos. `.gitattributes` itself travels via git to all clones.

resumePoint.description: Implementation complete. Both beads closed. Run pan done PAN-1901.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1901: bug(beads): .gitattributes declares 'merge=beads' but merge.beads.driver is never configured — .beads/.pan state still conflict-storms (PAN-1841 fix inert)

Completed both beads: (1) switched .beads/issues.jsonl to merge=union in .gitattributes with updated comments explaining the built-in driver and warning about single-JSON .pan/ files; (2) added 4 Vitest tests in gitattributes-merge.test.ts proving union merges cleanly, control confirms default 3-way conflicts, and two invariant guards block future regressions.

No deviations. Implemented both scoped beads: (1) switched .beads/issues.jsonl from inert merge=beads to built-in merge=union in .gitattributes, with comments explaining union requires no git config and warning single-JSON .pan/ state files must not use content-merge drivers; (2) added 4 Vitest tests in gitattributes-merge.test.ts proving union cleanly resolves divergent JSONL appends (control without union confirms default 3-way produces conflict markers), plus two invariant guards blocking re-introduction of inert custom drivers or content-merge on .pan/ JSON state files.
